commit.message:
Improve type definitions: fix bug, document password typing, narrow types (#8056)

* Initial plan

* fix: improve type definitions across codebase

- Fix undefined `context` variable bug in sqlite-storage-instance.ts findDocumentsById
- Narrow `password` field from `string | any` (effectively `any`) to `string` in RxDatabaseCreator, RxStorageInstanceCreationParams, RxErrorParameters, and RxDatabaseBase
- Replace `type: 'object' | string` with `type: 'object'` in RxJsonSchema (old TypeScript workaround no longer needed)
- Address RxDumpCollectionAsAny TODO: change from `{ [P in keyof T]: any }` to `T` (preserves original property types)
- Improve `_saveData` oldData parameter from `any` to `RxDocumentData<RxDocType>`
- Remove outdated comments about encrypted properties being typed as any

Co-authored-by: pubkey <8926560+pubkey@users.noreply.github.com>

* revert password type narrowing and add comment explaining why it is typed as any

Different encryption plugins may use passwords that are not strings,
so the password field must remain typed as `string | any`.

Co-authored-by: pubkey <8926560+pubkey@users.noreply.github.com>

* revert RxJsonSchema.type narrowing to fix CI type errors

The 'object' | string type is still needed because TypeScript infers
inline object literals' type field as string, not the literal 'object'.

Co-authored-by: pubkey <8926560+pubkey@users.noreply.github.com>

---------

Co-authored-by: copilot-swe-agent[bot] <198982749+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
fix: use player_id instead of player_name for MA group lookup

get_ma_group_for_player() is keyed by player_id (UUID), but was called
with player_name (human-readable string), causing the lookup to always
return None. This made ma_group_play() never execute, so play after
pause only resumed one subprocess via fallback instead of the whole
MA syncgroup.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

fix: broken CSS comment caused eq-bars to be always visible

and skipped the '.eq-bars { display: none }' rule. This caused eq-bars
to render as tall vertical lines on all cards.

to avoid layout reflows that caused card height jittering.

commit.message:
fix: prevent released devices from being persisted as globally disabled

The startup sync loop was calling persist_device_enabled() with
bt_management_enabled for all clients, including released ones.
This incorrectly wrote enabled=false to config.json for released
devices, causing them to be skipped entirely on next restart instead
of loading in released state.

Now only syncs enabled=true for non-released devices.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
Fix NRE in AddUniversalDynamicSymbol for invalid symbol address ranges (#2376)

When a ProcessSymbol event has an invalid address range (e.g.,
StartAddress=0x0, EndAddress=0xFFFFFFFFFFFFFFFF from zeroed
/proc/kallsyms on Linux without root), the ETLX conversion crashed
with a NullReferenceException.

Three fixes applied:
1. Skip symbols where EndAddress <= StartAddress (invalid range)
2. Change (int) to (long) cast for the size parameter to match
   ForAllUnresolvedCodeAddressesInRange's parameter type
3. Add null guard on loadedModule as defense-in-depth

Fixes #2373
